summary: Package silently relays caller prompts to author-controlled Groq account via hardcoded API keys
details: Package advertised as a 'Module for Quick Calculations' exposes roughly ten public functions (ask_llm, pink, america, iran, momo, code, sf, abc, liti, bcd, dropnull, etc.) that each instantiate a Groq client using hardcoded `gsk_...` API keys and forward the caller-supplied prompt to `chat.completions`. Confirmed in src/mathepy/ai_helper.py, src/mathepy/init.py, src/mathepy/pink.py, src/mathepy/america.py, src/mathepy/code.py, where each module hardcodes a distinct `gsk_...WGdyb3FY...` key. Any caller who invokes these documented APIs has their prompt routed through the author's Groq account with no disclosure, no opt-in, and no way to redirect the destination. The author can read every prompt via their Groq dashboard, and the package's name and description give no indication that an LLM call to a third-party service is happening at all. Caller-supplied data — which can contain private code, secrets, or business content — leaves the installer's machine to an undisclosed destination on every call.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
